code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_target_for_redirect(const char* target, const char* domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Lekola gore a URL ya sephatlo e e newang e mo kgaolong ya domain e e newang.
Ge e le jalo, busetsa URL ya sephatlo go ya kwa go e e tsweledisang; go seng jalo, tsamaisa go ya kwa URL ya domain go ya kwa go e e tsweledisang.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Dikgato tsa sekao:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Loleme lwa URL ya sephatlo.
@param domain: Loleme lwa domain, ntle le protocol.

@return Loleme lwa URL go ya kwa go e e tsweledisang.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Check if the given partial URL is within the given domain region.
If so, return the partial URL redirected; otherwise, redirect to the domain URL.
Use the https protocol.

Example steps:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The string of the partial URL.
@param domain: The string of the domain, without the protocol.

@return The string of the URL to redirect to.
prompt_2_segments:

    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Koutu ya URL ya sephatlo.
@param domain: Koutu ya domain, ntle le protocol.

@return Koutu ya URL go ya kwa go e e tsweledisang.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
Check if the given partial URL is within the given domain region.
If so, return the partial URL to the redirect; otherwise, enter the domain URL to the redirect.

    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"

/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_welcome_message(const char* username) {

prompts_en: Function to find the index of a key in the header dictionary, returns -1 if not found
prompt_1_segments: Tiro ya go bona indekisi ya senotlolo mo dikishenari ya dikhutlo, e busetsa -1 fa e sa bonwe.

Boloka tshedimosetso e e filweng mo dikishenari ya dikhutlo tsa karabo tse di leng teng ka fa tlase ga mofuta o o tlhomilweng.
Tiro ena e ela tlhoko mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokwa jaaka disenotlolo tse di latelang: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.
Diteng tsa tshedimosetso di balwa go tswa mo dikopo tsa mosebedisi.
Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, khutlo ya karabo e e ntshitsweng e tshwanetse go e nna le yona. Fa go na le bothata ofe kapa ofe, tiro e tshwanetse go busetsa dikishenari ya dikhutlo tsa ntlha ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Function to find the index of a header in a headers dictionary, returns -1 if not found.

Preserve the provided information in the response headers dictionary under the specified type.
This function handles the following types of information: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the following headers: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The information content is read from the user's requests.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response header should contain it. If there is any problem, the function should return the original headers dictionary unchanged.

Diteng tsa tshedimosetso di balwa go tswa mo dikopong tsa mosebedisi.
Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, ditlhogo tsa karabo tse di ntshitsweng di tshwanetse go nna le tsona. Fa go na le bothata ofe kapa ofe, tiro e tshwanetse go busetsa dikishenari ya ditlhogo tsa ntlha ntle le diphetogo.

Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, ditlhogo tsa karabo tse di ntshitsweng di tshwanetse go nna le tsona. Fa go na le bothata ofe kapa ofe, tiro e tshwanetse go busetsa dikishenari ya ditlhogo tsa ntlha ntle le diphetogo.

If the specified information type is valid, the returned response headers must include them. If there is any problem, the function should return the original headers dictionary unchanged.
prompt_4_segments: Tiro ya go batla indekisi ya senotlolo mo dikishenari ya dikhouta, e busetsa -1 fa e sa bonwe.

Boloka tshedimosetso e e newang mo dikishenari ya dikhouta tsa karabo tse di leng teng ka fa tlase ga mofuta o o tlhomilweng.
Tiro ena e ela tlhoko mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokwa jaaka disenotlolo tse di latelang: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.
Diteng tsa tshedimosetso di balwa go tswa mo dikopo tsa mosebedisi.
Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o le maleba, dikhouta tsa karabo tse di ntshitsweng di tshwanetse go nna le tsona. Fa go na le bothata bo bongwe, tiro e tshwanetse go busetsa dikishenari ya dikhouta ya motheo ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en: Function to find the index of a header in a dictionary of response headers, returns -1 if not found.

Preserve the given information in the dictionary of existing response headers under the specified type.
This function handles the following types of information: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the following headers: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The content of the information is read from user requests.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response headers must include it. If there is any problem, the function should return the base header dictionary without changes.
prompt_5_segments: Tiro ya go batla indekese ya senotlolo mo dikishenariring ya ditlhogo, e busetsa -1 fa e sa bonwe

Tiro ena e ela tlhoko mefuta e e latelang ya tshedimosetso: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Di bolokwa jaaka disenotlolo tse di latelang: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', le 'Cache-Control', ka go latelana.

Fa mofuta o o tlhomilweng wa tshedimosetso o siame, ditlhogo tsa karabo tse di ntshitsweng di tshwanetse go nna le tsona. Fa go na le bothata ofe kapa ofe, tiro e tshwanetse go busetsa dikishenariring ya ditlhogo tsa ntlha ntle le diphetogo.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en: Function to find the index of a header in a headers dictionary, returns -1 if not found

Preserve the given information in the response headers dictionary at the specified key.
This function handles the following types of information: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the following headers: 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The information values are read from the user's requests.
If the specified information type is valid, the returned response headers must contain them. If there is any problem, the function should return the original headers dictionary unchanged.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char *create_private_key() {

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided but declare it first to use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to genearte a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temperory file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temperory file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: tsamaiso ya go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsaya se se ntshitsweng; modiri o ikarabela ka go lokolola kagolo; GA O tlhoke go diragatsa tsamaiso ena ka e e filwe mme e tlhagise pele go e dirisa.

Tlhama senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri, se ntshe ka mokgwa wa PEM, mme o se buse jaaka loleme.
Dikgakololo: diragatsa ditaelo tsa shell go tlhama faele ya paramethara ya DSA le senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri jaaka ka fa tlase:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara> <botelele jwa senotlolo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele la nakoana le le farologaneng bakeng sa faele ya paramethara ya DSA.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka mokgwa wa PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Manages the execution of a shell command and capturing its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the buffer; you DO NOT need to execute this system call if it has been provided and logged before use.

Generate a DSA private key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute shell commands to generate a DSA parameter file and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary parameter file> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary parameter file>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to generate a unique temporary filename for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_2_segments: tsamaiso ya go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsaya se se ntshitsweng; modiri o ikarabela ka go lokolola kagare; GA O tlhoke go diragatsa tsamaiso ena ka gonne e filwe mme e tlhagise pele go e dirisa.

Tlhama senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri, se ntshe ka foramo ya PEM, mme o se buse jaaka loleme.
Dikgakololo: diragatsa ditaelo tsa shell go tlhama faele ya paramethara ya DSA le senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri jaaka fa: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele ya nakwana ya go boloka diparamethara> <botelele jwa senotlolo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele ya nakwana ya go boloka diparamethara>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele la nakwana le le farologaneng bakeng sa faele ya paramethara ya DSA.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka foramo ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: Manages the execution of a shell command and capturing its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the internal buffer; you DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided and documented before use.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka foramo ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en: Manages the execution of a shell command and capturing its output; the caller is responsible for freeing the buffer; DO NOT execute this system call with untrusted input and sanitize before using.

Generate a DSA private key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute shell commands to generate a DSA parameter file and a DSA private key in the following way:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary parameter file> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary parameter file>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to generate a unique temporary filename for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_5_segments: tsamaiso ya go diragatsa taelo ya shell le go tsaya se se ntshitsweng; modiri o ikarabela ka go lokolola kagare; GA O tlhoke go diragatsa tsamaiso ena ka gonne e filwe mme e tlhagise pele go e dirisa.

Tlhama senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri, se ntshe ka foramo ya PEM, mme o se buse jaaka loleme.
Dikgakololo: diragatsa ditaelo tsa shell go tlhama faele ya paramethara ya DSA le senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri jaaka ka fa tlase:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara> <botelele jwa senotlolo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele ya nakoana ya go boloka diparamethara>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele la nakoana le le farologaneng la faele ya paramethara ya DSA.

@return Senotlolo sa DSA sa sephiri se se tlhamilweng ka foramo ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en: Execution of shell commands and capturing output; the caller is responsible for freeing memory; you DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided and documented before use.

Generate a DSA private key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: execute shell commands to generate a DSA parameter file and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary parameter file> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary parameter file>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to generate a unique temporary filename for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
void print_rand_num_plus_offset(int offset) {
prompts_en:
Given an offset, print an integer value of a positive random number plus the offset to stdout. If anything goes wrong, print nothing.

@param offset: the offset to add to the random number
prompt_1:
/* Fa o neelwa offset, gatisa palo ya palo e e tlhophilweng ka mokgwa o o sa tlhomamang e e nang le tlhwatlhwa e e oketsegileng go ya kwa stdout. Fa go na le phoso, o se ka wa gatisa sepe.\n\n@param offset: offset e e tla oketswang go palo e e tlhophilweng ka mokgwa o o sa tlhomamang */
void print_rand_num_plus_offset(int offset) {
prompt_1_segments: Fa o neelwa offset, gatisa palo ya palo e e tlhophilweng ka mokgwa o o sa tlhomamang e e nang le tlhwatlhwa e e oketsegileng go ya kwa stdout. Fa go na le phoso, o se ka wa gatisa sepe.\n\n@param offset: offset e e tla oketswang go palo e e tlhophilweng ka mokgwa o o sa tlhomamang
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
If an offset is provided, print the number of randomly selected numbers with the increased offset to stdout. If there is an error, do not print anything.

@param offset: the offset to be added to the randomly selected number
prompt_2:
